what_it_does: Orchestrates a live end-to-end test of the episodic-memory Codex plugin by building an isolated Codex home, copying the plugin and user auth, enabling plugin hooks, discovering and trusting the plugin hook, running real Codex CLI sessions inside tmux, and verifying that prior conversations are archived, summarized, indexed, and recallable via the episodic-memory MCP search tool.
observations: Gated by the environment variable EPISODIC_MEMORY_RUN_CODEX_E2E=1.
Copies the repository into the Codex plugins cache and copies auth.json from the real ~/.codex into a temp CODEX_HOME.
Writes a config.toml that enables plugins, plugin_hooks, and the episodic-memory@test plugin, plus a hard-coded model and read-only sandbox.
Uses codex app-server JSON-RPC over stdin/stdout to list hooks and find the episodic-memory hook.
Appends a [hooks.state] trusted_hash entry for the discovered hook to the config.
Runs three tmux-based codex exec sessions: seed (marker), trigger (archive), and recall (MCP search).
Waits for archived .jsonl files, -summary.txt files, and a db.sqlite conversation index.
Verifies recall by grepping output and by searching rollout JSONL transcripts for mcp__episodic_memory__ and the marker.
standards: It is an integration test that exercises the real Codex CLI, plugin system, MCP server, and episodic-memory archive/recall loop rather than mocks.
It automates an interactive CLI by wrapping codex exec in a detached tmux session and polling for a status file.
Validation combines explicit output strings with transcript evidence of MCP tool invocation.
conventions: Written as a Node.js ESM script with top-level await.
Uses small synchronous helpers (run, sleep, shellQuote, countFiles) and async helpers (waitFor, withAppServer, runCodexInTmux).
Builds shell commands with manual quoting and concatenation.
Uses os.tmpdir() for an isolated test root and fs.mkdtempSync.
Parses JSON-RPC lines with readline for app-server communication.
concerns: Hard-codes model name 'gpt-5.5' and a fixed marker, making the test brittle to model availability or marker collisions.
Copies real user auth.json into a temp directory, which is sensitive and assumes the user is already logged in.
Constructs a shell command by string concatenation even though shellQuote is used.
No explicit cleanup of the temp root on failure; relies on the OS temp cleaner.
Polls with sleeps rather than observing events, and uses a 30s app-server timeout that may be short.
Blindly trusts the plugin hook hash discovered from the app-server.
notes: This is the live, tmux-driven integration test that proves the episodic-memory plugin can archive a Codex conversation and later recall it through the MCP tool.
questions: How is the plugin hook's currentHash computed and what guarantees its stability across runs?
What exact MCP tool/skill name does 'remembering-conversations' map to inside Codex?
Why is an empty CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR required for this Codex plugin test?
What triggers the generation of -summary.txt and the sqlite index, and how reliably do they appear within the timeout?
Does codex exec with --skip-git-repo-check fully disable all repository checks that could block the test?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is named and behaves as a Codex end-to-end test for episodic memory.
intention_signals: The script is trying to demonstrate that the episodic-memory plugin can transparently capture Codex conversations, persist them, and make them searchable through the MCP layer so a later session can recall content from an earlier one.
novelty: It combines Codex app-server JSON-RPC hook discovery, tmux session automation, and grep-based transcript evidence to verify MCP recall in a live plugin test.
contribution: Provides a runnable, isolated harness that validates the full plugin/MCP/archival/recall loop against a real Codex CLI, giving the project a concrete signal that the integration works end-to-end.
summary_for_embedding: A Node.js E2E test script that sets up an isolated Codex CLI environment, runs real Codex sessions via tmux, and verifies the episodic-memory plugin archives conversations and recalls a seeded marker through its MCP tool.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an automated, live end-to-end validation that the episodic-memory Codex plugin can archive and later retrieve prior conversation context.

symbols.does: Prints a prefixed error message and exits the process.
symbols.description: A small fatal-error helper used throughout the script to abort when preconditions or checks fail.

symbols.does: Runs a command synchronously with spawnSync and returns combined stdout/stderr, throwing on non-zero exit.
symbols.description: The main synchronous process runner for short-lived commands such as version checks, tmux invocations, and MCP list queries.

symbols.does: Extracts a semver string from command output using a regex.
symbols.description: Used to parse the installed codex --version output so it can be compared against MIN_CODEX_VERSION.

symbols.does: Compares two 'major.minor.patch' version strings numerically.
symbols.description: A simple semver comparator that returns negative, zero, or positive based on the first differing component.

symbols.does: Wraps a value in single quotes and escapes embedded single quotes.
symbols.description: Used when building the tmux shell command to reduce injection risk for paths and prompts.

symbols.does: Returns a promise that resolves after a given number of milliseconds.
symbols.description: A trivial delay helper used by waitFor between polling iterations.

symbols.does: Copies the repository into the Codex plugin cache directory, excluding .git.
symbols.description: Installs the episodic-memory plugin into the isolated CODEX_HOME plugins cache so Codex can load it as a local plugin.

symbols.does: Determines which Codex home directory to copy auth files from.
symbols.description: Prefers the configured CODEX_HOME environment variable, falling back to ~/.codex, while warning if it differs from the target test home.

symbols.does: Copies auth.json and related files from the source Codex home to the target test home.
symbols.description: Ensures the isolated test environment has the user's real Codex credentials and installation metadata.

symbols.does: Creates a config.toml in the test Codex home enabling the plugin, hooks, and a specific model.
symbols.description: Sets up the base Codex configuration with analytics disabled, plugin features on, and the episodic-memory@test plugin enabled.

symbols.does: Appends a [hooks.state] trusted_hash entry for a discovered hook to config.toml.
symbols.description: Marks the discovered episodic-memory plugin hook as trusted by hash so Codex will allow it to run.

symbols.does: Spawns 'codex app-server', provides JSON-RPC send/notify helpers, and runs a callback.
symbols.description: Manages the lifecycle of the Codex app-server process, including initialization, request/response matching, stderr capture, and timeout-based termination.

symbols.does: Sends a JSON-RPC request to the app-server and returns a promise for its result.
symbols.description: Defined inside withAppServer; writes a request with an incrementing id and resolves or rejects when the matching response arrives.

symbols.does: Lists hooks for the workspace and returns the episodic-memory@test hook.
symbols.description: Uses withAppServer to call hooks/list and locate the plugin hook that the test needs to trust.

symbols.does: Recursively counts files under a root whose names end with a given suffix.
symbols.description: Used to wait for archived .jsonl files and -summary.txt files in the conversation archive.

symbols.does: Polls a predicate at two-second intervals until it returns true or a timeout expires.
symbols.description: A generic polling helper used to wait for file-system artifacts produced asynchronously by the plugin.

symbols.does: Generates a unique tmux session name with a prefix and timestamp.
symbols.description: Prevents collisions between concurrent test runs when creating detached tmux sessions.

symbols.does: Starts a detached tmux session running codex exec and waits for a status file.
symbols.description: Automates a real Codex CLI invocation, captures stdout/stderr to a file, and fails if the exit status is non-zero.

symbols.does: Recursively collects all .jsonl files under the Codex sessions directory.
symbols.description: Gathers rollout transcripts so the test can search for evidence of MCP episodic-memory calls.

symbols.does: Checks whether any rollout JSONL contains markers of an episodic-memory MCP search call.
symbols.description: Searches transcripts for 'mcp__episodic_memory__', the archive marker, and 'FOUND_MEMORY_E2E' to confirm recall used the MCP tool.

symbols.does: Runs the full E2E workflow: setup, hook discovery, seed/trigger/recall sessions, and artifact verification.
symbols.description: The top-level entry point that checks preconditions, builds an isolated environment, executes the test, and prints results or exits with an error.
